criteria.name: Mathematical Accuracy
criteria.description: Correct formulation of the gradient descent update rule: θ = θ - α∇L(θ). Proper use of gradient, learning rate, and parameter notation.

criteria.name: Conceptual Understanding
criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of WHY gradient descent works — moving in the direction that decreases the loss. Understands the role of the learning rate.

criteria.name: Completeness
criteria.description: Covers the key aspects: update rule, convergence, learning rate effects (too large / too small). Mentions local vs global minima for non-convex functions.

criteria.name: Clarity
criteria.description: The explanation is organized and easy to follow. Concepts are introduced in a logical order.
